title: Measles Outbreak in England Leads to Two Child Deaths
subtitle: Health officials warn that vaccination rates are falling as new cases continue to rise across the country.

summary.en: Two children in England have died from measles this year, marking a tragic development in the ongoing outbreak. Health authorities report over 100 new cases in the last two weeks alone. Most of those affected are children aged 10 and under who have not received the necessary vaccines. Experts are calling for urgent action to improve vaccination coverage. The situation highlights the serious risks of a disease that was once considered under control.
summary.zh: 今年英格蘭有兩名兒童死於麻疹，這是該國持續爆發疫情以來悲劇性的發展。衛生當局報告顯示，僅在過去兩週內就新增了一百多起病例。受影響者多為十歲及以下未接種必要疫苗的兒童。專家呼籲採取緊急行動以提升疫苗接種覆蓋率。此情況凸顯了一種曾被認為已受控制的疾病所帶來的嚴重風險。

paragraphs.en: Health officials in England have confirmed a tragic development in the country’s ongoing measles outbreak. According to The Guardian Health, two children have died from the virus this year. One child died from acute measles, while the other passed away due to the late effects of the illness. This marks the first time since 2018 that two children have died from measles in a single year in England and Wales.
paragraphs.zh: 英國衛生官員證實該國持續爆發的麻疹疫情出現了悲劇性的發展。根據《衛報健康版》（The Guardian Health）報導，今年已有兩名兒童死於該病毒。其中一名兒童死於急性麻疹，另一名則因該疾病的後遺症而不幸離世。這是自二〇一八年以來，英格蘭與威爾斯地區首次出現一年內有兩名兒童死於麻疹的情況。

paragraphs.en: Data released by the UK Health Security Agency (UKHSA) shows that the virus continues to spread in many parts of the country. In the last two weeks, there have been 106 new cases confirmed by laboratories. This brings the total number of cases between January 1 and June 8 to 736. For comparison, there were 959 total cases recorded during the entire year of 2025. The highest numbers of new infections have been reported in London, the east of England, and the West Midlands.
paragraphs.zh: 英國衛生安全局（UKHSA）發布的數據顯示，該病毒持續在英國多地蔓延。在過去兩週內，實驗室已確認新增一百零六起病例。這使得今年一月一日至六月八日期間的病例總數達到七百三十六例。作為對比，二〇二五年全年的病例總數為九百五十九例。目前新增感染人數最多的地區為倫敦、英格蘭東部以及西密德蘭地區。

paragraphs.en: Dr. Vanessa Saliba, a consultant epidemiologist at the UKHSA, expressed deep sadness for the families who lost their children. She emphasized that measles is a highly infectious viral illness that can lead to serious health problems, such as pneumonia, brain inflammation, and in rare cases, long-term disability or death. Dr. Saliba urged all parents to ensure their children are up to date with their MMR or MMRV vaccines. These vaccines provide the safest protection against the disease. She also noted that anyone who has missed their shots can catch up at a local GP practice, regardless of their age.
paragraphs.zh: 英國衛生安全局的顧問流行病學家凡妮莎．薩利巴（Vanessa Saliba）博士對失去孩子的家庭表達了深切的哀悼。她強調，麻疹是一種傳染性極強的病毒性疾病，可能導致嚴重的健康問題，例如肺炎、腦炎，在極少數情況下還可能造成長期殘疾或死亡。薩利巴博士敦促所有家長確保其子女已按時接種麻疹、腮腺炎、德國麻疹（MMR）或麻疹、腮腺炎、德國麻疹、水痘（MMRV）疫苗。這些疫苗能提供對抗該疾病最安全的保護。她也指出，任何錯過接種的人，無論年齡大小，都可以前往當地的家庭醫師診所補打疫苗。

paragraphs.en: Most of the recent cases have been found in unvaccinated children aged 10 and under. The latest data shows that only 91.8% of five-year-olds in England had received one dose of the MMR vaccine in the 2024-25 period. This figure is the lowest level seen since 2010-11. Health experts and members of parliament have previously criticized the NHS, suggesting that it is failing to ensure children receive these vital vaccinations. Some reports have even compared vaccination rates in parts of England to those in countries like Afghanistan and Malawi.
paragraphs.zh: 近期大多數病例皆發現於十歲及以下未接種疫苗的兒童身上。最新數據顯示，在二〇二四至二〇二五年度，英格蘭僅有百分之九十一點八的五歲兒童接種了一劑麻疹、腮腺炎、德國麻疹疫苗。此數據為二〇一〇至二〇一一年度以來的最低水準。衛生專家與國會議員先前曾批評英國國家醫療保健服務（NHS），認為其未能確保兒童獲得這些關鍵疫苗的接種。部分報導甚至將英格蘭部分地區的疫苗接種率與阿富汗及馬拉威等國家相提並論。

paragraphs.en: James Murray, the health secretary, described these deaths as a heartbreaking reminder that measles is not a harmless childhood illness. He stated that the MMR vaccine remains the best protection available and has saved countless lives. Murray encouraged parents and carers to check their children’s vaccination status immediately. He explained that getting vaccinated does more than just protect the individual child; it also helps safeguard the most vulnerable people in the community, including babies who are too young to receive the vaccine and those with specific health conditions.
paragraphs.zh: 衛生大臣詹姆斯．莫瑞（James Murray）形容這些死亡案例是一個令人心碎的提醒，說明麻疹並非無害的兒童疾病。他表示，麻疹、腮腺炎、德國麻疹疫苗依然是目前最有效的保護措施，且已拯救了無數生命。莫瑞鼓勵家長與照護者立即檢查孩子的疫苗接種狀態。他解釋說，接種疫苗不僅能保護兒童個人，還有助於守護社區中最脆弱的群體，包括年紀太小而無法接種疫苗的嬰兒，以及患有特定健康狀況的人士。

paragraphs.en: The situation has become a significant public health concern. Earlier this year, officials noted that the UK is no longer considered to have eliminated measles, as vaccination coverage has stalled while cases continue to increase. As the virus continues to circulate, the focus remains on closing the gap in vaccination rates to prevent further tragedy. Health authorities are clear: the best way to stop the spread is to ensure that as many people as possible are protected through vaccination. Parents are being asked not to delay and to book an appointment with their doctor as soon as possible to ensure their families are safe.
paragraphs.zh: 目前的情況已成為重大的公共衛生隱憂。今年稍早，官員指出英國已不再被視為消除了麻疹，因為疫苗接種覆蓋率停滯不前，而病例數卻持續增加。隨著病毒持續傳播，當前的重點仍是縮小疫苗接種率的差距，以防止更多悲劇發生。衛生當局立場明確：阻止疫情擴散的最佳方式，是確保盡可能多的人透過接種疫苗獲得保護。當局呼籲家長切勿拖延，應儘速與醫師預約，以確保家人的安全。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To stop making progress or to come to a standstill.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見於描述引擎熄火，這裡指疫苗接種率停止成長。文中：as vaccination coverage has stalled while cases continue to increase.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To do something that you did not have time to do earlier.
keyTerms.noteZh: 這裡指補打之前錯過的疫苗。文中：She also noted that anyone who has missed their shots can catch up at a local GP practice, regardless of their age.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To reduce the difference between two things, such as current levels and target levels.
keyTerms.noteZh: 這裡指縮小疫苗接種率與目標之間的落差。文中：As the virus continues to circulate, the focus remains on closing the gap in vaccination rates to prevent further tragedy.

sources.name: The Guardian Health
sources.title: Two children die from measles as England data shows 100 new infections

previousArticles.title: Measles Outbreak Nears Worst Levels in Decades
previousArticles.summaryEn: The United States is currently facing a major measles outbreak with over 2,000 confirmed cases this year. Experts warn that the actual number of infections is likely much higher due to limited tracking. The virus is spreading quickly in communities where vaccination rates have fallen over the last two decades. Public health departments are struggling to manage the crisis because of limited funding and political challenges. Doctors are now calling for better leadership and clearer communication to help protect vulnerable children.

quiz.question: How do the measles-related deaths reported this year differ from those in previous years according to the article?

options.A: This is the first time children have died from measles since 2018.
options.B: This is the first time in over a decade that more than one child has died in a single year.
options.C: These are the first recorded deaths caused by the late effects of the measles virus.
options.D: These deaths represent the highest number of measles fatalities ever recorded in England.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。文章第一段明確指出，這是自 2018 年以來，英格蘭和威爾斯首次在一年內有兩名兒童死於麻疹。選項 B 雖然提到兩名兒童死亡，但文章並未強調這是「十年來」首次。選項 C 錯誤，因為文章僅提到其中一名兒童死於麻疹的後遺症，並未稱這是「首次」有此類死因。選項 D 誇大了事實，文章中並未將此數據與歷史最高紀錄進行比較。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the current vaccination situation in England based on the provided text?

options.A: The NHS has successfully eliminated measles through mandatory vaccination programs.
options.B: Vaccination rates are currently higher than they were during the 2010-11 period.
options.C: The decline in vaccination coverage is a major factor contributing to the resurgence of the virus.
options.D: Parents are intentionally refusing vaccines due to a lack of trust in medical professionals.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章第六段提到英國已不再被視為「消除麻疹」的國家，且疫苗接種率停滯不前，導致病例增加，可推論接種率下降與疫情復燃有直接關聯。選項 A 與文章第六段內容矛盾。選項 B 錯誤，因為第四段明確指出 2024-25 年的接種率是自 2010-11 年以來最低的。選項 D 雖然提到接種率低，但文章並未說明家長拒絕接種的具體動機，故無法斷定是「缺乏信任」。

quiz.question: In the sixth paragraph, what does the word 'stalled' most closely mean in the context of vaccination coverage?
quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。在文章第六段中，「stalled」用來形容疫苗接種覆蓋率的停滯狀態，意指進展停滯或無法提升。選項 A 與原意相反。選項 C 是該詞在機械故障情境下的常見解釋，但在文中不適用。選項 D 並非該詞的含義，文章中也未提到需要更多研究來解決此問題，而是強調需要採取行動補足接種缺口。

quiz.question: What is the primary message of this article?

options.A: To criticize the government for comparing England to developing nations.
options.B: To explain the medical symptoms of measles in children.
options.C: To highlight the urgent need for increased vaccination to combat the rising measles outbreak.
options.D: To report on the personal stories of families affected by the virus.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。全文的核心在於報導麻疹疫情的嚴重性，並透過專家呼籲和數據分析，強調提升疫苗接種率是遏止疫情擴散的關鍵。選項 A 僅是文章中的一個細節，並非全文主旨。選項 B 僅在第三段簡短提到，並非報導重點。選項 D 雖然提到悲劇，但文章重點在於公共衛生政策與預防，而非深入報導個別家庭的個人故事。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
